questions.text: Which tRNA can bind at mRNA Condon UAC?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The codon is a sequence of three bases on mRNA that determines an amino acid. The anticodon is a sequence of tRNA that is complementary to the codon. From the question, first, write the antiparallel sequence for the codon based on the tRNA anticodon. In this example, the anticodon is UAC. Thus, the codon would be AUG.

questions.text: If a man of M blood group marries a woman of N blood group, all their children will have.
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: There are three general phenotypes; M,N and MN. M phenotype has antigen M which is produced by gene LM. N phenotype has antigen N that is produced by its allele LN. MN phenotype has both M and N antigens, simultaneously produced by their alleles LM and LN

questions.text: Which of the following structures produce the fluid protein of semen?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Seminal vesicles, Bulbourethral gland, Prostrate gland
options.explanation: Accessory glands of the male reproductive system individually contribute to the composition of seminal plasma. The seminal vesicles produce the majority of seminal plasma and most notably, provide fructose as an energy source for ATP production

questions.text: What does the cat show when it runs for its food dish upon hearing the food can opener?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Insight learning is a sudden realization of a solution to a problem. Because of its suddenness, insight learning is different from trial-and-error learning, which requires more careful experimentation to arrive at a solution.

A hungry animal will respond to a situation in a way that is most natural for that animal. If one of these responses leads to the reward of food, it is likely that the specific response which led to the food reward will be repeated and thus learned.

Habituation, or the relatively permanent waning of a response as a result of repeated stimulation, is a form of behavioural plasticity that allows animals to filter out irrelevant stimuli and to focus selectively on important stimuli.

Imprinting refers to a well-researched phenomenon within animals where they form an extremely close and dependent bond with the first animal they see after being born. For example, when ducklings hatch, they tend to imprint on their parent and follow them everywhere.

questions.explanation: A hungry animal will respond to a situation in a way that is most natural for that animal. If one of these responses leads to the reward of food, it is likely that the specific response which led to the food reward will be repeated and thus learned.

questions.text: What type of heat exchange is promoted by vasodilation
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Vasodilation brings more blood and heat to the body surface, facilitating radiation and evaporative heat loss, which helps to cool the body. Vasoconstriction reduces blood flow in peripheral blood vessels, forcing blood toward the core and the vital organs found there, and conserving heat.

Vasodilation brings more blood and heat to the body surface, facilitating radiation and evaporative heat loss, which helps to cool the body. Vasoconstriction reduces blood flow in peripheral blood vessels, forcing blood toward the core and the vital organs found there, and conserving heat.

Vasodilation brings more blood and heat to the body surface, facilitating radiation and evaporative heat loss, which helps to cool the body. Vasoconstriction reduces blood flow in peripheral blood vessels, forcing blood toward the core and the vital organs found there, and conserving heat.

Vasoconstriction reduces blood flow in peripheral blood vessels, forcing blood toward the core and the vital organs found there, and conserving heat.

questions.text: All of the following contain cartilage except
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The larynx is a cartilaginous skeleton, some ligaments and muscles that move and stabilize it, and a mucous membrane. The laryngeal skeleton has nine cartilages: the thyroid cartilage, cricoid cartilage, epiglottis, arytenoid cartilage, corniculate cartilage, and cuneiform cartilage.

The trachea and extrapulmonary bronchi are composed of hyaline cartilage, fibrous tissue, muscular fibers, mucous membrane, and glands. The tracheal cartilages form incomplete C-shaped rings that occupy the anterior two thirds of the trachea.

The trachea and extrapulmonary bronchi are composed of hyaline cartilage, fibrous tissue, muscular fibers, mucous membrane, and glands. The tracheal cartilages form incomplete C-shaped rings that occupy the anterior two thirds of the trachea.
Bronchioles are a conducting division of the respiratory system and it does not contain cartilage.

questions.explanation: Bronchioles are a conducting division of the respiratory system and it does not contain cartilage.

questions.text: A trait whose alleles are present in both male and female but expresses more in one sex than other
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Sex-linked, as related to genetics, refers to characteristics (or traits) that are influenced by genes carried on the sex chromosomes. In humans, the term often refers to traits or disorders influenced by genes on the X chromosome, as it contains many more genes than the smaller Y chromosome.

Sex-influenced traits are autosomal traits that are influenced by sex. If a male has one recessive allele, he will show that trait, but it will take two recessive for the female to show that same trait. One such gene is baldness. A lot of sex-limtied traits can determine parental carriers by using a pedigree.

Sex-limited traits are determined by genes located on autosomes and express only in one sex. While these traits are responsible considerably for sexual dimorphism, sex-influenced traits do not show distinctive expression between women and men. Numerous analytical models have treated sex as an essential factor.

For a trait to be considered Y linkage, it must exhibit these characteristics: occurs only in males. appears in all sons of males who exhibit that trait. is absent from daughters of trait carriers; instead the daughters that are phenotypically normal and do not have affected offspring.

questions.explanation: Sex-linked, as related to genetics, refers to characteristics (or traits) that are influenced by genes carried on the sex chromosomes. In humans, the term often refers to traits or disorders influenced by genes on the X chromosome, as it contains many more genes than the smaller Y chromosome.

questions.text: Organism of same species inhabiting in space and time form a
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A population is a group of organisms of the same species that live in the same area at the same time.
A biological species is a group of organisms that can reproduce with one another in nature and produce fertile offspring.

Community, in biology, an interacting group of various species in a common location. For example, a forest of trees and undergrowth plants, inhabited by animals and rooted in soil containing bacteria and fungi, constitutes a biological community. food web. Category: Animals & Nature. Also called: biological community.
The biosphere is the region of the earth that encompasses all living organisms: plants, animals and bacteria. It is a feature that distinguishes the earth from the other planets in the solar system.

questions.explanation: A population is a group of organisms of the same species that live in the same area at the same time.

questions.text: The present giraffe has a long neck compared to its ancestors, Lamarck believed it could be due to
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Natural selection is a mechanism of evolution. Organisms that are more adapted to their environment are more likely to survive and pass on the genes that aided their success. This process causes species to change and diverge over time.

The inheritance of acquired characteristics is a theory that physiological changes acquired during an organism's lifetime can be passed down to the offspring. It is also known as adaptation theory, and its sometimes confused with Lamarckism, the evolutionary theory of French naturalist Jean-Baptiste Lamarck.

There are two main ways to isolate organisms. Streaking for isolation on an agar plate involves the successive dilution of organisms until you have the cells at a low enough density that single cells are physically isolated spatially to give rise to recognizable individual colonies.

Developmental biology specification refers to the process in which cells and genes that belong to particular species develop from their initial stages to the point where they perform particular functions.

questions.explanation: The inheritance of acquired characteristics is a theory that physiological changes acquired during an organism's lifetime can be passed down to the offspring. It is also known as adaptation theory, and its sometimes confused with Lamarckism, the evolutionary theory of French naturalist Jean-Baptiste Lamarck.

questions.text: Crumpled leaves like structure are found in_ stage of xerarch succession
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A crust is any external protective surface and crustose means crusts on the substratum. Special types of lichens get impregnated in the form of crust. They can live in extreme conditions.
In this stage the lichens are just like crumpled leaves attached at one point. It produces shade to the crustose lichens as a result of which their growth is reduced or decreased.

This is the third stage with mosses like, Polytrichum, Tortula etc. They compete with lichens for water and penetrate much deeper into the soil as compared to the lichens, adding more humus to the soil.
Small seedling of herbaceous plants now establish due to the more availability of moisture, humus and soil for anchorage.

questions.explanation: In this stage the lichens are just like crumpled leaves attached at one point. It produces shade to the crustose lichens as a result of which their growth is reduced or decreased.

questions.text: Myoglobin occurs in
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Myoglobin is an oxygen binding protein present in all muscle fibers. It is an iron-containing protein similar to haemoglobin and gives the muscle a reddish appearance. Red muscle fibres have abundant myoglobin while white muscle fibers have less myoglobin.

questions.text: Na+ - K+ ATPase pump is important for
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Na,K-ATPase maintains the proper concentration gradients of Na+ and K+. It is important that Na,K-ATPase stabilizes the resting membrane potential. If the resting membrane potential of nerve cells is not stabilized, nerve cells may fire when not needed or may not fire at all.

questions.text: Specific sequences of four or six nucleotides arranged symmetrically in reverse order where restriction enzymes work are known as
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Okazaki fragments are short sequences of DNA nucleotides (approximately 150 to 200 base pairs long in eukaryotes) which are synthesized discontinuously and later linked together by the enzyme DNA ligase to create the lagging strand during DNA replication.

In molecular biology, palindromic sequences are referred to as the sequence of nucleotides in the DNA duplex or RNA, where the sequence in one strand is the same as the complementary sequence of the other strand when read from the same direction on both the strands, either 5' to 3' or 3' to 5'.

Promoter sequences define the direction of transcription and indicate which DNA strand will be transcribed; this strand is known as the sense strand. Many eukaryotic genes have a conserved promoter sequence called the TATA box, located 25 to 35 base pairs upstream of the transcription start site.

A primer is a short nucleic acid sequence that provides a starting point for DNA synthesis. In living organisms, primers are short strands of RNA. A primer must be synthesized by an enzyme called primase, which is a type of RNA polymerase, before DNA replication can occur.

questions.explanation: In molecular biology, palindromic sequences are referred to as the sequence of nucleotides in the DNA duplex or RNA, where the sequence in one strand is the same as the complementary sequence of the other strand when read from the same direction on both the strands, either 5' to 3' or 3' to 5'.

questions.text: During PCR which enzyme is used to do polymerization of DNA fragments
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: RNA polymerase is a multi-unit enzyme that synthesizes RNA molecules from a template of DNA through a process called transcription. The transcription of genetic information into RNA is the first step in gene expression that precedes translation, the process of decoding RNA into proteins. Jonathan Dornell, PhD.

DNA polymerases are enzymes that create DNA molecules by assembling nucleotides, the building blocks of DNA. These enzymes are essential to DNA replication and usually work in pairs to create two identical DNA strands from one original DNA molecule.

Like DNA replication in an organism, PCR requires a DNA polymerase enzyme that makes new strands of DNA, using existing strands as templates. The DNA polymerase typically used in PCR is called Taq polymerase, after the heat-tolerant bacterium from which it was isolated (Thermus aquaticus).
DNA ligases are enzymes required for the repair, replication and recombination of DNA. DNA ligases catalyse the formation of phosphodiester bonds at single-strand breaks in double-stranded DNA.

questions.explanation: Like DNA replication in an organism, PCR requires a DNA polymerase enzyme that makes new strands of DNA, using existing strands as templates. The DNA polymerase typically used in PCR is called Taq polymerase, after the heat-tolerant bacterium from which it was isolated (Thermus aquaticus).

questions.text: The pancreas releases which of the following hormones
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: They are secreted by adrenal glands
They are released by pituitary glands
They are secreted by thyroid glands.

The main hormones secreted by the endocrine gland in the pancreas are insulin and glucagon, which regulate the level of glucose in the blood, and somatostatin, which prevents the release of insulin and glucagon.

questions.explanation: The main hormones secreted by the endocrine gland in the pancreas are insulin and glucagon, which regulate the level of glucose in the blood, and somatostatin, which prevents the release of insulin and glucagon.
